data.title: Unilever Plans Executive Pay Overhaul to Boost Performance and Attract U.S. Talent
data.description: Unilever is planning to revamp its executive compensation strategy to better incentivize exceptional performance and attract more talent from the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's efforts to align its pay structure with its growth objectives. The move aims to enhance Unilever's competitive edge by rewarding top executives who drive significant business growth and innovation. This initiative is part of Unilever's broader strategy to strengthen its leadership team and expand its presence in the U.S. market. The company believes that a more attractive compensation package will help in recruiting and retaining high-caliber executives who can contribute to its long-term success.

data.title: BNB Chain Leads in ERC-8004 AI Agent Deployment
data.description: BNB Chain has surpassed Base and Ethereum in hosting ERC-8004 AI agents, with a total of 34,278 agents out of 89,451 registered. According to NS3.AI, the overall number of ERC-8004 agents across various blockchain networks has increased significantly, rising from 337 at the beginning of the year to nearly 130,000.

data.title: Institutional Investors Increase US Dollar Purchases Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: Institutional investors are purchasing US dollars at the highest rate in almost two years, driven by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the demand for safe-haven assets has surged as geopolitical tensions escalate. State Street reports that this trend reflects investors' preference for stability amid global uncertainties.

data.title: U.S. Commercial Paper Market Sees Mixed Movements Amid Financial Uncertainty
data.description:
The Federal Reserve's recent data release indicates a mixed performance in the U.S. commercial paper market for the week ending March 11. According to Jin10, seasonally adjusted figures show an increase of $13 billion in outstanding commercial paper, reaching a total of $1.41 trillion. However, the unadjusted balance saw a decline of $9.5 billion, bringing it to $1.423 trillion.

Some analysts suggest that the unadjusted data may provide a more accurate reflection of market conditions, particularly in light of ongoing financial uncertainties. The unadjusted outstanding balance of U.S. foreign financial commercial paper also decreased by $20 billion, settling at $376.3 billion. These movements highlight the complexities and challenges facing the commercial paper market amid broader economic concerns.

data.title: Explosion Reported in Tehran as Air Defense Systems Activate
data.description: An explosion was reported in Tehran, the capital of Iran, at approximately 22:10 local time on the 12th. According to Jin10, the city's air defense systems were activated following the incident. Further details about the cause and impact of the explosion are yet to be disclosed.

data.title: U.S. Interior Secretary to Discuss LNG Agreements in Japan
data.description: U.S. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum is set to discuss liquefied natural gas (LNG) purchase agreements during his visit to Japan this week. According to Jin10, Burgum emphasized that the United States aims to promote more LNG purchase agreements from Alaska.

data.title: Bitcoin Policy Institute Advocates for Broader Crypto Tax Relief
data.description: The Bitcoin Policy Institute (BPI) has called on Congress to extend proposed de minimis crypto tax relief beyond payment stablecoins to include bitcoin. According to NS3.AI, lawmakers are currently considering thresholds of $200 and $300. BPI argues that the existing property treatment of bitcoin makes every purchase a taxable event, which discourages small payments and remittances. Over the past three months, the group has engaged with 19 congressional offices, advocating for a broader exemption that could allow up to $600 per transaction with an annual cap of approximately $20,000.

data.title: Nvidia to Invest $26 Billion in AI Models to Enhance Efficiency
data.description: Nvidia has announced the release of Nemotron 3 Super and plans to invest $26 billion over the next five years in developing frontier open-weight AI models. According to NS3.AI, this initiative is designed to enhance the efficiency of autonomous AI agents and address China's growing influence in the open-source AI sector.

data.title: Bitmine Acquires 30,000 ETH in Major Transaction
data.description: Bitmine has completed a significant purchase of 30,000 ETH, valued at approximately $61.89 million, from FalconX. According to NS3.AI, FalconX is the cryptocurrency brokerage that facilitated this transaction. This acquisition highlights Bitmine's strategic move in the cryptocurrency market, reflecting ongoing interest and investment in Ethereum.

data.title: Oil Price Surge May Increase Investment in Argentina's Shale Sector
data.description: Higher oil prices resulting from the conflict in Iran may lead to increased capital expenditure in Argentina's shale industry, according to Vista CEO Miguel Galuccio. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Galuccio's remarks on the potential impact of global oil market dynamics on Argentina's energy sector. The country's shale production is already experiencing growth, and the rise in oil prices could further stimulate investment and development in this area. Galuccio emphasized the importance of capitalizing on the current market conditions to enhance Argentina's energy output and infrastructure.

data.title: U.S. Trade Representative and Treasury Secretary to Visit Paris for Trade Talks
data.description: U.S. Trade Representative Katherine Tai and Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen are scheduled to visit Paris from March 15 to 16 for discussions on trade relations. According to Jin10, the visit aims to strengthen economic ties between the United States and France. The meetings will focus on addressing trade barriers and exploring opportunities for collaboration in various sectors. This visit underscores the ongoing efforts to enhance bilateral trade and economic cooperation between the two nations.

data.title: Iraq to Resume Oil Exports via Turkey Amid Persian Gulf Disruptions
data.description: Iraq is expected to soon resume its oil exports through Turkey, following disruptions caused by the Iran conflict that led to the closure of its southern export terminals in the Persian Gulf. According to Jin10, the resumption of oil transportation is anticipated to stabilize the regional oil supply chain, which has been affected by geopolitical tensions. The closure of the terminals had significant implications for Iraq's oil export capacity, impacting global oil markets. The reopening of the route through Turkey is seen as a crucial step in restoring Iraq's export capabilities and mitigating the effects of the ongoing conflict on the energy sector.

data.title: Iran Declares 'National Unity Week' Following Leader's Call for Solidarity
data.description: Iran's First Vice President, Arif, announced on March 12 that the upcoming week will be designated as 'National Unity Week.' According to Jin10, this decision was inspired by the first public statement from Supreme Leader Mujtaba Khamenei regarding national unity. Arif urged citizens to actively participate in street demonstrations to showcase Iran's national solidarity, Islamic authority, and support for Supreme Leader Mujtaba.

data.title: Judge Dismisses Majority of Lawsuit Over Gateway Tunnel Funding
data.description: A judge has dismissed the majority of a lawsuit against the Trump administration concerning the freezing of funds allocated for the Gateway tunnel project under the Hudson River. Bloomberg posted on X that the decision follows the U.S. government's release of the disputed payments, allowing work to continue on the $16 billion infrastructure project. The Gateway tunnel is a significant undertaking aimed at improving transportation links between New York and New Jersey. The lawsuit had challenged the administration's decision to halt funding, which was seen as a critical component for the project's progress. With the funds now released, construction efforts have resumed, marking a pivotal moment for the development of the tunnel.

data.title: U.S. Senate Blocks Homeland Security Funding Bill in Test Vote
data.description: The U.S. Senate has once again rejected the Homeland Security funding bill in a test vote. According to Jin10, the vote concluded with a result of 51 to 46, falling short of the 60 votes required for passage. This decision reflects ongoing challenges in securing bipartisan support for the legislation, which aims to address critical funding needs for national security operations. The Senate's inability to advance the bill underscores the complexities of legislative negotiations and the political dynamics at play in Washington.

data.title: UK Confirms Downing of Iranian Drones in Iraq, Injuring US Soldiers
data.description: On March 13, Jin10 reported that UK Defense Secretary John Healy confirmed the downing of two Iranian drones by British forces stationed in Iraq. According to Jin10, the incident occurred on March 12, with the drone wreckage falling in a joint military base in Erbil, the capital of Iraq's Kurdistan region. The event resulted in injuries to several U.S. soldiers. The situation highlights ongoing tensions in the region and the involvement of international forces in maintaining security.

data.title: Suspected Gunman Killed in Michigan Synagogue Incident
data.description: A suspected gunman was killed in an incident at a synagogue in Michigan, according to U.S. law enforcement sources. The individual, armed with a rifle, drove into the building and engaged in a shootout with armed security personnel. According to Jin10, the vehicle caught fire, resulting in the suspect's body being burned. The identity of the suspect has not yet been confirmed.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 70,000 USDT with a 1.20% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 12, 2026, 18:21 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 70,000 USDT and is now trading at 69,944.257813 USDT, with a narrowed 1.20% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Authorities Dismantle SocksEscort Proxy Network, Freeze $3.5 Million in Cryptocurrency
data.description: European and U.S. authorities have successfully dismantled the SocksEscort proxy network, freezing $3.5 million in cryptocurrency connected to the operation. According to NS3.AI, the crackdown targeted a service that Europol reported had compromised over 369,000 routers and Internet of Things devices across 163 countries. In addition to freezing assets, authorities seized 34 domains and 23 servers spanning seven countries.

data.title: FBI Warns of Potential Iranian Drone Attack on California
data.description: The FBI issued a warning last month to law enforcement agencies about a potential drone attack by Iran on California. According to Jin10, this alert was in response to possible U.S. actions against Iran. White House Press Secretary Levitt stated on Thursday that the warning was based on unverified information. Levitt clarified, 'There is no such threat from Iran to our homeland, and it has never occurred.'
